name: Waianae
desc: Dramatic coastline, deep poverty — stunning scenery, strong Hawaiian community, visible homelessness.
insider: Waianae Boat Harbor is where local fishermen sell fresh ahi, ono, and mahi straight off the boat in the early morning — bring cash and a cooler
The mountain backdrop of the Waianae Range is the most dramatic scenery on Oahu — Makua Valley at the north end is considered sacred Hawaiian land and access is restricted
Do not leave any valuables in your car — this is the number one rule of the entire Leeward Coast and it applies double at beach parks and trailheads
Tamura's Waianae has excellent poke and is the best-stocked market on this stretch of coast — it's where everyone does their main grocery shopping
Pokai Bay Beach Park has a protected cove that's one of the calmest swimming spots on the west side — the ancient Kuilioloa Heiau sits on the point above
The homelessness situation along the coast is visible and significant — encampments line sections of Farrington Highway and beach parks
Kaena Point Trail from the Waianae side is a flat, stunning coastal hike to Oahu's westernmost tip where monk seals rest and albatross nest
The community is deeply Hawaiian with active cultural practitioners — you'll hear Hawaiian language, see traditional fishing practices, and feel a connection to the land
Waianae Coast Comprehensive Health Center provides medical, dental, and social services and is a crucial community anchor for the entire Leeward Coast
Makaha through Waianae has produced some of Hawaii's greatest watermen and surfers — this coast has a surf heritage that rivals the North Shore in depth if not in fame
